messages.text: Heads up — tc_reset_rr_17 failed in tonight's regression. Can someone take a look?
I saw that. Honestly, I think the checker is being too strict here. We had a similar situation last year with the legacy arbiter and there was an exception for reset behavior. Let me dig up the JIRA.
Found it — JIRA-4721. There was a reset exception that allowed the arbiter to start from any port after reset in certain conditions. I think this might apply here too.
Hmm, that was for the legacy arbiter though, right? Not sure about the details.
The underlying principle should be the same. Reset is messy — you can't always guarantee deterministic state. I'd lean toward relaxing the checker rather than changing RTL this close to tapeout.
Let's not jump to conclusions. @jordan can you do a proper triage? Check the log, waveform, RTL, and spec. I want to know the actual root cause before we decide anything.
Sure, but I'm 80% sure it's just the checker. The RTL has been stable for months.
On it. Will report back with full triage.

BTW, I just remembered — when I wrote the reset logic, I was referencing an older version of the spec. But honestly, I still think the checker is the problem. The RTL works fine in silicon on the previous chip.
